description: Full Seaman Reborn game - sardonic creature cognition with local LLM, persistent memory, evolving personality, real-time care mechanics, Pygame visual interface, audio I/O, and UE5-ready API bridge

userStories.title: Initialize git repo and Python project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project structure in place so all future stories have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: git repo initialized with .gitignore
pyproject.toml with all dependencies defined
src/seaman_brain/ package structure created with __init__.py files
tests/ directory structure mirrors src/
config/ directory with default.toml and stage overrides
data/ directory with .gitkeep files
python -m seaman_brain --version prints version
ruff check passes with zero errors
pytest collects successfully
userStories.notes: Scaffold complete. Build backend is setuptools.build_meta. pytest-asyncio uses asyncio_mode=auto.

userStories.title: Implement shared types and enums
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared type definitions so all modules use consistent data structures.
acceptanceCriteria: CreatureStage enum with 5 stages: MUSHROOMER, GILLMAN, PODFISH, TADMAN, FROGMAN
MessageRole enum with SYSTEM, USER, ASSISTANT
ChatMessage dataclass with role, content, timestamp fields
MemoryRecord dataclass with text, embedding, timestamp, importance, source fields
All types have proper type hints and are importable from seaman_brain.types
At least 3 tests covering enum values, dataclass creation, and edge cases
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement TOML config loader with Pydantic models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed configuration loading so modules can access settings safely. Must include config sections for all subsystems: llm, memory, personality, creature, cli, audio, environment, needs, gui, api.
acceptanceCriteria: SeamanConfig Pydantic model aggregating all sub-configs
LLMConfig with provider, model, temperature, max_tokens, base_url fields
MemoryConfig with buffer_size, vector_dims, top_k, extraction_interval fields
PersonalityConfig with base_traits dict and stages_path
AudioConfig with tts_provider, stt_provider, tts_model, stt_model fields
EnvironmentConfig with tank defaults (temperature, cleanliness, oxygen)
NeedsConfig with hunger_rate, comfort_decay, health thresholds
GUIConfig with window_width, window_height, fps, theme
APIConfig with host, port, cors_origins
load_config() function reads config/default.toml and returns SeamanConfig
Stage-specific TOML files can override personality traits
At least 3 tests: default loading, missing file handling, override merging
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement LLM provider abstract protocol
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a provider contract so LLM backends are interchangeable.
acceptanceCriteria: LLMProvider Protocol class with async chat() and async stream() methods
chat() takes list[ChatMessage] and returns str
stream() takes list[ChatMessage] and returns AsyncIterator[str]
Protocol is runtime-checkable
At least 3 tests verifying protocol compliance with a mock implementation
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement Ollama LLM provider
userStories.description: As a user, I need local LLM inference via Ollama so the creature can think without cloud APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: OllamaProvider class implementing LLMProvider protocol
Configurable model name, temperature, and context window
async chat() sends messages to Ollama and returns response text
async stream() yields response chunks for streaming display
Handles connection errors gracefully with clear error messages
At least 3 tests with mocked Ollama client
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement OpenAI LLM provider
userStories.description: As a user, I need an OpenAI cloud fallback for when local inference is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenAIProvider class implementing LLMProvider protocol
Reads API key from OPENAI_API_KEY env var or config
async chat() and stream() work with OpenAI API format
Graceful handling of missing API key with helpful error message
At least 3 tests with mocked OpenAI client
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement Anthropic LLM provider
userStories.description: As a user, I need an Anthropic cloud fallback for when local inference is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: AnthropicProvider class implementing LLMProvider protocol
Reads API key from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var or config
Converts ChatMessage format to Anthropic's system+messages format
async chat() and stream() work with Anthropic API
Graceful handling of missing API key with helpful error message
At least 3 tests with mocked Anthropic client
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement LLM provider factory
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a factory so the orchestrator can create providers from config.
acceptanceCriteria: create_provider(config: LLMConfig) -> LLMProvider factory function
Selects provider based on config.provider string ('ollama', 'openai', 'anthropic')
Validates provider availability before returning
Raises clear error for unknown provider names
At least 3 tests covering each provider selection and error case
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement episodic memory buffer
userStories.description: As a creature, I need short-term conversation memory so I can maintain context within a session.
acceptanceCriteria: EpisodicMemory class with configurable max_size (default 20)
add(message: ChatMessage) appends to buffer
Buffer automatically evicts oldest messages when full
get_recent(n: int) returns last n messages
get_all() returns all messages in order
clear() empties the buffer
At least 3 tests: add/retrieve, overflow eviction, edge cases
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement embedding provider with Ollama embeddings
userStories.description: As a developer, I need text embeddings so memories can be stored and searched as vectors.
acceptanceCriteria: EmbeddingProvider class wrapping Ollama embeddings API
Configurable model name (default: all-minilm:l6-v2)
async embed(text: str) -> list[float] returns embedding vector
async embed_batch(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]] for batch processing
Handles empty strings and very long texts gracefully
At least 3 tests with mocked Ollama embed calls
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement LanceDB semantic memory store
userStories.description: As a creature, I need persistent long-term memory so I remember the human across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: SemanticMemory class using LanceDB for persistent vector storage
Configurable database path (default: data/lancedb/)
async add(record: MemoryRecord) stores memory with embedding
async search(vector: list[float], top_k: int) returns similar memories
Auto-creates table schema on first use
At least 3 tests with temporary database directory
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement hybrid memory retriever
userStories.description: As a creature, I need intelligent memory recall that balances relevance with recency.
acceptanceCriteria: HybridRetriever class combining semantic similarity and temporal recency
async retrieve(query: str, top_k: int) returns ranked MemoryRecords
Configurable weights for similarity vs recency scoring
Handles empty memory store gracefully (returns empty list)
Re-ranks results by weighted combination score
At least 3 tests with mocked semantic memory and embeddings
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement memory extraction pipeline
userStories.description: As a creature, I need to automatically extract and store facts from conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: MemoryExtractor class using LLM to extract facts from conversation
async extract(messages: list[ChatMessage]) -> list[str] extracts key facts
async extract_and_store(messages, memory_store) extracts and persists
Extraction prompt focuses on: user preferences, personal info, topics, observations
Configurable extraction interval (every N messages)
At least 3 tests with mocked LLM and memory store
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement personality trait system
userStories.description: As a creature, I need configurable personality traits so my behavior changes with each evolutionary stage.
acceptanceCriteria: 8 trait dimensions as floats 0.0-1.0: cynicism, wit, patience, curiosity, warmth, verbosity, formality, aggression
TraitProfile dataclass holding all 8 traits
Default TraitProfile for each CreatureStage
Traits loadable from stage-specific TOML config files
Trait values clamp to [0.0, 1.0] range
At least 3 tests: default profiles, stage loading, clamping
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement personality constraints and output filtering
userStories.description: As a creature, I need output filtering so I never break character with AI assistant cliches.
acceptanceCriteria: FORBIDDEN_PHRASES list of AI assistant cliches to strip
apply_constraints(response: str, profile: TraitProfile) -> str filters output
Strips phrases like 'As an AI...', 'I would be happy to...', 'I cannot...'
Enforces max response length based on verbosity trait
Handles empty or whitespace-only responses
At least 3 tests: phrase stripping, length enforcement, edge cases
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement system prompt builder
userStories.description: As a creature, I need a dynamic system prompt so the LLM knows how to roleplay me at each stage. This is the MOST CRITICAL file for achieving authentic Seaman personality. Must include creature identity, stage behavior, trait-driven tone, remembered facts, mood modifiers, time-of-day awareness, and strict negative constraints suppressing AI assistant tone.
acceptanceCriteria: PromptBuilder class assembling the system prompt for LLM calls
build(stage, traits, memories, creature_state) -> str returns system prompt
Prompt incorporates: creature identity, stage-specific behavior, trait-driven tone
Includes remembered facts about the user as context
Includes creature mood and trust level modifiers
Prompt clearly instructs LLM to stay in character as Seaman
Strict negative constraints: no AI filler, no solicitous follow-ups, no breaking character
At least 3 tests: basic build, with memories, different stages
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement creature state and persistence
userStories.description: As a creature, I need persistent state so I survive between sessions. State includes evolutionary stage, biological needs, mood, trust, interaction history, and timestamps.
acceptanceCriteria: CreatureState dataclass with stage, age, interaction_count, mood, trust_level, hunger, health, comfort, last_fed, last_interaction, birth_time
StatePersistence class for JSON save/load to data/saves/
save(state: CreatureState, path: str) writes JSON file
load(path: str) -> CreatureState reads and validates JSON
Creates backup before overwriting existing save
Handles missing save file by returning default state
At least 3 tests: save/load roundtrip, missing file, backup creation
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement creature evolution logic
userStories.description: As a creature, I need to evolve through 5 stages based on interaction and trust.
acceptanceCriteria: EvolutionEngine class managing stage transitions
check_evolution(state: CreatureState) -> CreatureStage | None
Evolution conditions: interaction count thresholds, trust level, time elapsed
Stage order: MUSHROOMER -> GILLMAN -> PODFISH -> TADMAN -> FROGMAN
evolve(state, new_stage) updates state and triggers trait profile change
Cannot skip stages or devolve
At least 3 tests: no evolution, threshold reached, max stage
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement context assembler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need context assembly so the LLM receives a well-structured message array.
acceptanceCriteria: ContextAssembler class combining all context for LLM calls
assemble(system_prompt, episodic_messages, retrieved_memories) -> list[ChatMessage]
System prompt goes first as SYSTEM message
Retrieved memories formatted and injected as context
Episodic messages appended in chronological order
Manages total token budget across components
At least 3 tests: basic assembly, with memories, budget limits
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement conversation manager (main orchestrator)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a central orchestrator that coordinates all subsystems into a conversation loop.
acceptanceCriteria: ConversationManager class orchestrating the full conversation flow
async process_input(user_input: str) -> str is the main entry point
Flow: add to episodic -> update state -> check evolution -> retrieve memories -> build prompt -> assemble context -> LLM chat -> filter -> store -> save
Handles graceful degradation when subsystems unavailable
async initialize() sets up all subsystems from config
async shutdown() cleanly closes connections and saves state
At least 3 tests with mocked subsystems: happy path, degradation, evolution trigger
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement terminal CLI interface
userStories.description: As a user, I need a terminal interface so I can talk to the creature interactively.
acceptanceCriteria: Terminal UI using rich for output and prompt_toolkit for input
Displays creature stage and mood indicators in header
Streaming response display as LLM generates tokens
Debug commands: /state, /memory, /stage, /traits, /reset, /quit
Handles Ctrl+C gracefully (saves state before exit)
python -m seaman_brain launches the interactive terminal
At least 3 tests: command parsing, display formatting, graceful shutdown
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Integration tests for brain core conversation loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I need end-to-end tests proving the brain core works together.
acceptanceCriteria: Full end-to-end test with mocked LLM provider
Test: user sends message, gets personality-appropriate response
Test: memory extraction triggers after N messages
Test: creature evolves after reaching interaction threshold
Test: state persists across simulated sessions (save/load)
Test: graceful degradation when embedding service unavailable
All integration tests pass with pytest -m integration
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement TTS voice synthesis provider
userStories.description: As a creature, I need a voice so the human can hear me speak. Implement a pluggable TTS provider abstraction with a pyttsx3 (offline, cross-platform) default and hooks for future Piper/Kokoro integration. TTS runs on CPU to preserve GPU for LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: TTSProvider Protocol with async synthesize(text: str) -> bytes and speak(text: str) -> None
Pyttsx3TTSProvider as default offline implementation
Configurable voice, rate, and volume from AudioConfig
Async wrapper so TTS doesn't block the main loop
Graceful fallback to no-audio mode if TTS unavailable
At least 3 tests with mocked TTS engine
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement STT speech recognition provider
userStories.description: As a user, I need to speak to the creature with my microphone. Implement a pluggable STT provider with SpeechRecognition (offline Vosk or online Google) as default, with hooks for future faster-whisper integration.
acceptanceCriteria: STTProvider Protocol with async listen() -> str (blocking until speech detected)
SpeechRecognitionSTTProvider wrapping the SpeechRecognition library
Configurable recognizer backend (vosk offline, google online) from AudioConfig
Handles microphone permission errors and no-mic gracefully
Returns empty string on timeout or unintelligible input
At least 3 tests with mocked microphone input
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement audio manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a unified audio manager coordinating TTS output, STT input, and sound effects playback for both terminal and GUI modes.
acceptanceCriteria: AudioManager class coordinating TTS, STT, and SFX
async speak(text: str) routes to TTS provider
async listen() -> str routes to STT provider
play_sfx(sound_name: str) plays sound effects from assets/sounds/
Supports enable/disable per channel (tts, stt, sfx)
Thread-safe for concurrent GUI usage
At least 3 tests: speak routing, listen routing, sfx playback
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement real-time clock and time awareness
userStories.description: As a creature, I need to know what time it is in the real world. The original Seaman was tied to the Dreamcast's real-time clock — the creature knew if you visited at 3am, commented on holidays, and starved if you didn't show up. Implement time awareness that feeds into the prompt builder and needs system.
acceptanceCriteria: GameClock class wrapping real-time clock with game-relevant properties
Properties: time_of_day (morning/afternoon/evening/night), day_of_week, is_weekend, hours_since_last_session, session_duration_minutes
get_time_context() -> dict returns all time data for prompt injection
Tracks session start/end timestamps persisted in creature state
Detects long absences (>24h, >48h, >7d) with severity levels
At least 3 tests: time-of-day detection, absence calculation, context dict format
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement tank environment state
userStories.description: As a creature, I live in a tank whose conditions affect my health. The tank has temperature (controllable by player), cleanliness (degrades over time), and oxygen level. During the Podfish->Tadman transition, the tank drains into a terrarium.
acceptanceCriteria: TankEnvironment dataclass: temperature, cleanliness, oxygen_level, water_level, environment_type (aquarium/terrarium)
update(elapsed_seconds: float) degrades cleanliness and oxygen over time
set_temperature(value: float) with min/max bounds per environment type
clean() restores cleanliness to 1.0
drain() transitions from aquarium to terrarium (required for Podfish->Tadman)
is_habitable() checks if conditions are survivable
Persistence: save/load with creature state
At least 3 tests: degradation over time, temperature bounds, drain transition
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement creature needs system
userStories.description: As a creature, I have biological needs that the human must attend to. Hunger increases over time (must be fed), comfort decreases with bad tank conditions, health degrades from unmet needs. Needs directly affect mood and can cause death if ignored.
acceptanceCriteria: CreatureNeeds dataclass: hunger (0-1, 0=full, 1=starving), comfort (0-1, 1=happy), health (0-1, 1=healthy), stimulation (0-1, 1=engaged)
NeedsEngine class with update(elapsed_seconds, tank_state, interaction_count) -> CreatureNeeds
Hunger increases at configurable rate (faster for higher stages)
Comfort derived from tank temperature and cleanliness
Health degrades when hunger > 0.8 or comfort < 0.2 for extended periods
Stimulation decays without interaction, restored by conversation
get_urgent_needs() -> list[str] returns needs requiring immediate attention
At least 3 tests: hunger growth, comfort from tank, health degradation
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement feeding mechanics
userStories.description: As a user, I need to feed the creature to keep it alive. Different food types have different effects. Overfeeding and underfeeding both have consequences. The Mushroomer stage requires feeding it the nautilus (tutorial).
acceptanceCriteria: FoodType enum: PELLET, WORM, INSECT, NAUTILUS (stage-specific)
FeedingEngine class with feed(creature_state, food_type) -> FeedingResult
FeedingResult dataclass: hunger_change, mood_change, health_change, message
Stage-appropriate food validation (Mushroomer only eats nautilus, etc.)
Overfeeding penalty (feeding when hunger < 0.1 causes discomfort)
Feeding cooldown to prevent spam-feeding
At least 3 tests: successful feed, wrong food for stage, overfeeding
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement tank care mechanics
userStories.description: As a user, I need to maintain the tank. Temperature must be kept in a species-appropriate range, the tank gets dirty over time and needs cleaning, and oxygen depletes. Neglecting the tank kills the creature.
acceptanceCriteria: TankCareEngine class coordinating all tank maintenance actions
adjust_temperature(delta: float) with stage-specific optimal ranges
clean_tank() restores cleanliness, takes time (not instant)
get_tank_warnings() -> list[str] returns urgent maintenance alerts
Tank state affects creature comfort and health via NeedsEngine
Stage-specific requirements (terrarium needs sprinkler for Frogman)
At least 3 tests: temperature adjustment, cleaning, warning generation
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement death and revival mechanics
userStories.description: As a creature, I can die from neglect — starvation, suffocation, temperature extremes, or prolonged illness. Death is consequential (the original Seaman permanently died). Implement death detection, death state, and optional revival (new egg) mechanic.
acceptanceCriteria: DeathEngine class monitoring creature health for death conditions
check_death(creature_state, needs, tank) -> DeathCause | None
DeathCause enum: STARVATION, SUFFOCATION, HYPOTHERMIA, HYPERTHERMIA, ILLNESS, OLD_AGE
Death triggers: hunger=1.0 for >1hr, oxygen<0.1, temp out of range for >30min, health=0
on_death(cause) saves death record, resets to new egg state
Death message varies by cause (sardonic even in death)
Optional grace period / warning system before death
At least 3 tests: starvation death, temperature death, revival to new egg
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement dynamic mood engine
userStories.description: As a creature, my mood should be a dynamic emergent property of my needs, recent interactions, trust level, time of day, and personality traits. Mood affects response tone and is displayed in the GUI.
acceptanceCriteria: CreatureMood enum: HOSTILE, IRRITATED, SARDONIC, NEUTRAL, CURIOUS, AMUSED, PHILOSOPHICAL, CONTENT
MoodEngine class with calculate_mood(needs, trust, time_context, recent_interactions, traits) -> CreatureMood
Mood weighted formula: needs satisfaction (30%), trust level (20%), interaction quality (20%), time factors (15%), personality baseline (15%)
Mood transitions are gradual (no instant jumps from HOSTILE to CONTENT)
get_mood_modifiers() -> dict returns prompt modifiers for current mood
At least 3 tests: mood from needs, mood transition smoothing, modifier generation
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement autonomous behavior system
userStories.description: As a creature, I should do things unprompted — swim around, tap the glass, make observations about the time, complain about hunger, react to the player's webcam presence. These idle behaviors make the creature feel alive between conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: BehaviorEngine class managing autonomous creature actions
IdleBehavior dataclass: action_type, message, animation_hint, priority
get_idle_behavior(creature_state, needs, mood, time_context) -> IdleBehavior | None
Behavior types: IDLE_SWIM, TAP_GLASS, COMPLAIN, OBSERVE, SLEEP, EAT
Behaviors weighted by mood and needs (hungry creature complains about food)
Cooldown system preventing repetitive behaviors
Optional: generate_idle_comment(behavior, llm_provider) for LLM-powered idle speech
At least 3 tests: behavior selection, cooldown enforcement, need-driven priorities
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement event system
userStories.description: As a creature, random and time-triggered events should occur. The original Seaman had events tied to real dates, breeding events, stage transitions with dramatic environmental changes. Events drive narrative progression.
acceptanceCriteria: EventSystem class managing scheduled and random events
GameEvent dataclass: event_type, trigger_condition, message, effects, one_shot
Event types: EVOLUTION_READY, BREEDING, HOLIDAY, MILESTONE, RANDOM_OBSERVATION, ENVIRONMENTAL
Time-triggered events (creature comments on holidays, weekends, 3am visits)
Stage-triggered events (Gillman cannibalism, Podfish mating, tank drain prompt)
register_event(event) and check_events(state, time) -> list[GameEvent]
Events can modify creature state, tank state, and trigger dialogue
At least 3 tests: time trigger, stage trigger, one-shot vs repeating
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement Pygame window and main game loop
userStories.description: As a user, I need a graphical window to see and interact with the creature. Set up the Pygame window with a proper game loop (input handling, update, render at configurable FPS), event system integration, and clean shutdown.
acceptanceCriteria: GameWindow class managing Pygame initialization and main loop
Configurable window size (default 1024x768) and FPS (default 30)
Main loop: handle_events() -> update(dt) -> render() -> clock.tick()
Pygame event routing to game subsystems (keyboard, mouse, window close)
Integration point for ConversationManager (async bridge)
Clean shutdown: saves creature state, closes Pygame, exits cleanly
python -m seaman_brain --gui launches the Pygame window
At least 3 tests: window creation, event routing, shutdown sequence
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement tank environment renderer
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see the creature's habitat. Render an aquarium with animated water, bubbles, gravel substrate, and background. Transitions to terrarium view when tank is drained. Visual indicators for temperature and cleanliness.
acceptanceCriteria: TankRenderer class drawing the tank environment on a Pygame surface
Aquarium mode: animated water surface, rising bubbles, gravel floor, blue-green tint
Terrarium mode: dry substrate, rocks, moisture particles, green tint
Visual temperature indicator (blue=cold, green=good, red=hot) as gradient overlay
Cleanliness affects water clarity (murky green when dirty)
Smooth transition animation between aquarium and terrarium
All rendering uses simple shapes/procedural art (no external sprite assets required)
At least 3 tests: aquarium render, terrarium render, temperature overlay
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement creature sprite and animation system
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see the creature in the tank. Each of the 5 evolutionary stages needs a distinct visual representation. Use procedural/geometric art (circles, ellipses, lines) to represent each stage — no external assets needed. Idle, swimming, talking, and eating animations.
acceptanceCriteria: CreatureRenderer class drawing the creature for each stage
5 stage renderers: Mushroomer (small spore with eye), Gillman (fish with human face), Podfish (fish with legs), Tadman (tadpole humanoid), Frogman (frog-man)
All art is procedural (Pygame draw calls) — no external image files required
Animation states: IDLE, SWIMMING, TALKING, EATING, SLEEPING
Smooth position interpolation as creature moves around tank
Talking animation: mouth movement synced to TTS or text output
Face tracks toward mouse cursor (simulating watching the player)
At least 3 tests: stage rendering, animation state transitions, position interpolation
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement chat panel overlay
userStories.description: As a user, I need to read and type messages within the Pygame window. Implement a semi-transparent chat panel at the bottom of the screen showing conversation history and a text input field.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatPanel class rendering conversation history and text input on Pygame surface
Semi-transparent dark overlay at bottom third of window
Scrollable message history with user messages right-aligned, creature left-aligned
Text input field with cursor, basic editing (backspace, delete, home, end)
Enter key sends message to ConversationManager
Streaming display: creature response appears character-by-character
Toggle visibility with Tab key (show/hide chat panel)
At least 3 tests: message rendering, text input handling, streaming display
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement HUD and status display
userStories.description: As a user, I need to see the creature's vital statistics. Display creature stage, mood, and need bars (hunger, health, comfort) as a HUD overlay. Show tank condition indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: HUD class rendering status information on Pygame surface
Top bar: creature name/stage, mood indicator (text + color), session timer
Need bars: hunger, health, comfort, stimulation as colored progress bars
Tank indicators: temperature gauge, cleanliness meter, oxygen level
Trust meter: visual representation of trust level (0-1)
Color coding: green=good, yellow=warning, red=critical for all metrics
Compact mode (icon-only) and expanded mode (icon + label + value)
At least 3 tests: bar rendering, color thresholds, mode switching
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement interactive elements (feeding, tank controls)
userStories.description: As a user, I need to interact with the tank using mouse clicks. Click to feed the creature, adjust temperature with a slider/buttons, tap the glass to get the creature's attention, and clean the tank.
acceptanceCriteria: InteractionManager class handling mouse-based tank interactions
Click on creature or tank drops food at click position (opens food selection menu)
Temperature control: up/down buttons or clickable thermometer on tank edge
Tap glass: clicking on tank walls triggers creature reaction (startled, annoyed, curious)
Clean tank button in HUD triggers cleaning animation and effect
Drain tank button appears when evolution requires environment transition
Visual feedback for all interactions (ripples on click, food falling animation)
At least 3 tests: feeding interaction, temperature control, glass tap response
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement audio integration in Pygame
userStories.description: As a user, I need to hear the creature and ambient environment sounds. Integrate TTS voice output, ambient water/terrarium sounds, UI sound effects, and optional STT microphone input within the Pygame game loop.
acceptanceCriteria: PygameAudioBridge class connecting AudioManager to Pygame mixer
Ambient sound loop: water bubbling (aquarium) or nature sounds (terrarium)
Creature voice: TTS output plays through Pygame mixer when creature speaks
UI SFX: button clicks, feeding splash, glass tap, evolution chime
Volume controls per channel (ambient, voice, sfx) via config
Microphone input toggle (press M or dedicated button to speak)
Handles missing audio hardware gracefully (visual-only fallback)
At least 3 tests: ambient loop control, voice playback routing, volume adjustment
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Integrate full Pygame game loop
userStories.description: As a user, I need all Pygame subsystems working together as a cohesive game experience. Wire up tank renderer, creature sprites, chat panel, HUD, interactions, audio, needs tick, mood updates, autonomous behaviors, and events into the main game loop.
acceptanceCriteria: GameEngine class orchestrating all Pygame subsystems
Game loop: process input -> update needs/mood/behaviors/events -> update animations -> render tank -> render creature -> render HUD -> render chat -> flip display
Async bridge: ConversationManager runs in background thread, results displayed in chat
Needs and tank state update every game tick (scaled by real elapsed time)
Autonomous behaviors trigger idle animations and optional speech
Events checked each tick, displayed as notifications or dialogue
Evolution transitions trigger visual and audio celebration sequence
Death triggers game-over screen with cause and option to restart (new egg)
At least 3 tests: full loop tick, async conversation bridge, evolution sequence
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement FastAPI WebSocket server for UE5 bridge
userStories.description: As a developer preparing for UE5 integration, I need a WebSocket API server that exposes the brain's capabilities over the network. UE5 will connect as a client, sending player input and receiving creature responses, state updates, and events.
acceptanceCriteria: FastAPI app with WebSocket endpoint at /ws/brain
REST endpoints: GET /api/state, GET /api/health, POST /api/reset
WebSocket protocol: JSON messages with type field (input, response, state_update, event)
Client sends {type: 'input', text: '...'}, server responds with {type: 'response', text: '...', state: {...}}
Server pushes unsolicited state updates (mood changes, need alerts, evolution events)
CORS configuration for local UE5 development
python -m seaman_brain --api launches the API server
At least 3 tests: WebSocket connection, message exchange, state endpoint
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement brain state protocol schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a well-defined JSON protocol schema so UE5 and any future client can communicate with the brain reliably. Define all message types, state shapes, and event formats.
acceptanceCriteria: Pydantic models for all protocol messages: InputMessage, ResponseMessage, StateUpdate, EventNotification
BrainStateSnapshot model: creature_state, needs, tank, mood, active_traits, current_stage
EventNotification model: event_type, severity, message, effects, timestamp
Protocol version field for future compatibility
Serialization/deserialization utilities with validation
Documentation comments explaining each field's purpose and value ranges
At least 3 tests: serialization roundtrip, validation of invalid messages, version check
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Implement real-time event streaming over WebSocket
userStories.description: As a UE5 client, I need to receive real-time updates about the creature's state — mood shifts, need changes, autonomous behaviors, evolution events, and death — so the 3D rendering can react instantly.
acceptanceCriteria: EventBroadcaster class that collects state changes and pushes to all connected WebSocket clients
Subscribable event channels: mood, needs, evolution, behavior, tank, death
State diff streaming: only sends changed fields, not full state each time
Configurable broadcast interval (default: 500ms for needs, instant for events)
Client can subscribe/unsubscribe to specific channels
Handles client disconnection gracefully without crashing the brain
At least 3 tests: event broadcasting, channel subscription, client disconnect handling
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Full game integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive end-to-end tests proving the entire system works: brain + GUI + audio + needs + events + API. Simulate a full play session from hatching through feeding, conversation, evolution, and death.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test: full conversation with mocked LLM through GUI bridge
Integration test: creature needs degrade over simulated time, feeding restores them
Integration test: evolution triggers when conditions met, stage changes propagate to all subsystems
Integration test: death triggers from neglect, game-over state reached
Integration test: API WebSocket client receives state updates and can send input
Integration test: save/load preserves creature state, tank state, and memories across sessions
All integration tests pass with pytest -m integration
ruff check passes, pytest passes
